original_hypothesis.title: Referral Fee on Sells Permanently Locked When No Referral Defined
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In _transferFees for sells, sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee. When no referral is defined (referralFeeDestination == address(0)), the referral .call is skipped with `(true, bytes(''))`. The referralFee ETH is deducted from the seller's payout but never sent to anyone, permanently locked in the Curves contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Most tokens likely have no referral set. Every sell on these tokens loses referralFee ETH permanently.

proof_of_signal.pattern: When !referralDefined, referral .call is (true, bytes('')), skipping transfer; but sellValue already deducted referralFee

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Asymmetric handling of missing referral between buy and sell
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: On buy: referralFee goes to protocol. On sell: referralFee goes nowhere — locked in contract
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No fallback destination for referral fee on sells when referral undefined

analysis_questions: What percentage of tokens have referral destinations set?
What is the cumulative stuck ETH from this over protocol lifetime?
validation_requirements: Sell tokens for a subject with no referral, verify contract balance increase includes referralFee

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not an attack but systematic loss. If referralFeePercent = 2.5% and sell price = 1 ETH, 0.025 ETH per sell is permanently locked. With no extraction mechanism, this accumulates indefinitely.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Cumulative ETH lockup from all sells on tokens without referral destinations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Sellers receive reduced payout; locked ETH is irrecoverable

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test verifying fee routing completeness on sells without referral

validation_result.mechanism: locked referral fee on sells without referral destination
validation_result.impact_summary: When no referral fee destination is set for a token subject, the referralFee ETH is deducted from the seller's payout during sells but never sent to anyone, permanently locking it in the Curves contract with no recovery mechanism.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _transferFees function handles referral fees asymmetrically between buys and sells. On buys, when no referral is defined, the referral fee is added to the protocol fee (buyValue = protocolFee + referralFee) and sent to protocolFeeDestination. On sells, sellValue always deducts referralFee (sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee), but the referral .call is skipped with (true, bytes('')) when no referral destination is defined. This means the referralFee ETH is subtracted from the seller's proceeds but never transferred to any address, remaining permanently locked in the Curves contract. There is no withdrawal or rescue function for stuck ETH in the contract.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal at Curves.sol lines 193-196 is accurate. The vulnerable pattern spans lines 180-196, showing the asymmetric handling. Line 183: sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee (always deducts referralFee). Lines 193-196: when !referralDefined, the ternary evaluates to (true, bytes('')), skipping the ETH transfer entirely. For buys (line 182), buyValue = referralDefined ? protocolFee : protocolFee + referralFee correctly redirects the referral fee to the protocol. No such fallback exists for sells.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: On sells (isBuy=false): sellValue deducts referralFee from seller payout on line 183. When referralDefined is false (lines 193-195), the referral .call evaluates to (true, bytes('')) — a no-op that sends zero ETH. The referralFee amount remains in the contract with no mechanism to extract it. Compare with buys where line 182 adds referralFee to buyValue when !referralDefined, correctly redirecting it to the protocol.

calling_sequence: 1. sellCurvesToken called by user selling tokens for a subject with no referral set
2. price = getPrice(supply - amount, amount) computes bonding curve sell price
7. sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee (referralFee deducted)
10. referralDefined is false => (true, bytes('')) — referralFee NOT sent to anyone
12. Result: referralFee ETH stays in Curves contract permanently
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The sell path at line 225 calls _transferFees at line 236. In _transferFees, line 183 computes sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - referralFee - holderFee, always deducting referralFee. Lines 193-195 check referralDefined: when false, the ternary returns (true, bytes('')) instead of sending ETH. The referralFee ETH is never sent anywhere. On buys (line 182), the equivalent case adds referralFee to buyValue sent to protocolFeeDestination, showing the developers intended a fallback but forgot to implement one for sells.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that set referralFeePercent or referral destinations. The defect is inherent in the contract logic regardless of configuration, as long as referralFeePercent > 0 and a subject has no referral destination set.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack but a systematic loss on every sell transaction where the subject has no referral destination. If referralFeePercent = 2.5% (25e15) and a sell price is 1 ETH, then 0.025 ETH per sell is permanently locked. Since most subjects likely never set a referral destination, this affects the majority of sell transactions. The locked ETH accumulates indefinitely with no recovery mechanism. Additionally, the protocolFee on sells is also locked (separate but related issue), compounding the problem.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Every sell transaction on a token subject without a referral destination loses the referralFee portion of ETH permanently. Sellers receive reduced payouts (sellValue has referralFee deducted) while the deducted amount stays in the contract. Over protocol lifetime, this represents significant cumulative ETH lockup. There is no admin function or mechanism to withdraw stuck ETH from the Curves contract.
recommended_mitigations: Add a fallback destination for referralFee on sells when no referral is defined, mirroring the buy behavior: when !referralDefined on sells, add referralFee to sellValue or send it to protocolFeeDestination

Alternatively, modify sellValue calculation to only subtract referralFee when referralDefined is true: uint256 sellValue = price - protocolFee - subjectFee - (referralDefined ? referralFee : 0) - holderFee
Also address the related issue: protocolFee on sells is never sent to protocolFeeDestination — it should be sent separately
Add an emergency ETH withdrawal function for the contract owner to recover any stuck funds
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Curves with referralFeePercent > 0. 2. Create a token subject WITHOUT setting referralFeeDestination. 3. Buy several tokens as different users. 4. Record Curves contract ETH balance. 5. Sell tokens and observe that sellValue = price - all fees including referralFee. 6. Verify Curves contract balance increased by protocolFee + referralFee (both stuck). 7. Confirm no mechanism to extract the stuck ETH.
uncertainties: The exact referralFeePercent configured in production deployment is unknown, affecting the magnitude of locked ETH per transaction
The proportion of subjects that set referral destinations vs. those that don't is unknown but presumed to be heavily skewed toward not setting one
additional_testing_needed: Foundry/Hardhat test: sell tokens for subject with no referral, assert contract balance increases by exactly protocolFee + referralFee
Verify there is no hidden ETH withdrawal mechanism in the contract
Quantify cumulative locked ETH under realistic trading scenarios
